description: The Islamic Iran Participation Front (Jebhe-ye Mosharekat-e Iran-e Eslami), abbreviated IIPF, was the largest and most prominent reformist political party in post-revolutionary Iran. Founded in 1998 by [Mohammad-Reza Khatami](/people/mohammad-reza-khatami), it was the main vehicle for the reformist 2nd Khordad movement that swept the 6th [Majlis](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) in 2000. The [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) barred all IIPF candidates from running in the 2004 parliamentary election, effectively eliminating the party from parliament. It was formally suspended by the government in 2010 following the [Green Movement](/events/green-movement-protests-2009) protests. The party advocated political pluralism, civil society, rule of law, and engagement with the West.
editorial_notes: Founded 1998 by Mohammad-Reza Khatami. All candidates barred from 2004 election by Guardian Council. Suspended by Interior Ministry in 2010.
